frameworks.name: successor representation
frameworks.one_line: predictive state representation bridging model-based and model-free value learning

frameworks.name: temporal difference learning
frameworks.one_line: TD value learning extended to predictive state features rather than rewards

frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: dynamic-programming evaluation contrasted as costlier decision-time alternative

frameworks.name: model-free reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: cached-value mechanism that successor representation partially subsumes

core_question: How can model-based behavior emerge from neural circuits built on model-free temporal difference learning?
key_assumptions: neural circuits supporting model-based and model-free RL overlap structurally
value can be decomposed into a predictive state representation times a learned reward vector
TD learning can update predictive features as well as scalar values
manipulations: simulated tasks contrasting successor representation against model-based and model-free baselines
introduction of two algorithmic extensions that progressively mitigate successor-representation limitations
key_findings: successor representation combined with TD yields a subset of model-based behaviors at lower decision-time cost
behavioral repertoire of the successor representation partially matches biological organisms
two new algorithms extend the successor representation to address its limitations
interpretations: model-based computation may be constructed on top of a TD-learning core via predictive representations
successor representation offers a unifying account linking model-free mechanisms to model-based behavior
limitations_noted: successor representation alone captures only a subset of model-based behaviors
